MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2019 Continued To Apr-29-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. CHRISTOPHER S. HICKS, ESQ. of Kern Segal & Murray (Tel: 415-474-1900) appearing for plaintiffs Susam Huang and Que Duong;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The matter is not contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants Hui Wu and Michael Young's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to all four causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Susan Huang and Que Duong is denied as to all four causes of action. Judge Chan's order filed May 29, 2018 does not bar any of the four claims alleged by Ms. Huang and Ms. Duong in this case either based on res judicata or collateral estoppel. Res judicata is inapplicable because this case does not involve either the same cause of action or remedy that was involved in the family law case. Collateral estoppel is inapplicable because none of the issues essential to plaintiffs prevailing on any of their claims in this case were conclusively litigated and decided in the family law case. Judge Chan decided that a restraining order was unwarranted, the request for a restraining order was filed with an ulterior motive, and Ms. Huang's testimony lacked credibility. Judge Chan did not, however, conclusively determine that no assault or abuse occurred. Liberally construing the complaint, plaintiffs have adequately alleged claims for assault, intentional infliction of emotional distress, and elder abuse, which are not uncertain, ambiguous, or unintelligible.

FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Not Reported (302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-28-2018 Continued To Feb-25-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
